data.title: KBRA Assigns Preliminary Ratings to OBX 2026-AHC1 Trust
data.description: In New York, credit rating agency KBRA has assigned preliminary ratings to 62 classes of mortgage pass-through notes from OBX 2026-AHC1 Trust, a prime agency-eligible RMBS transaction sponsored by Onslow Bay Financial LLC. The transaction, fully originated and serviced by AmeriHome Mortgage Company, LLC, consists of 692 residential mortgages with an aggregate unpaid principal balance (UPB) totaling around $349.3 million as of the April 1, 2026 cut-off date.

data.title: Revision Skincare® CMT Post‑Procedure Cream™ Named 2026 NewBeauty Award Winner
data.description: In Irving, Texas, on April 3, 2026, Revision Skincare® revealed that their CMT Post-Procedure Cream™ has won a 2026 NewBeauty Award in the In-Office/Post-Procedure category. The prestigious honor signifies the cream's effectiveness and success in the industry. The NewBeauty Awards annually showcase exceptional products, with the cream standing out among others in its category.

data.title: Leading Parkinson's Organizations Urge Congress to Preserve Progress and Strengthen Parkinson's Research Funding at the National Institutes of Health
data.description: In New York on April 3, 2026, the leading national Parkinson's advocacy organizations appealed to Congress to carefully evaluate the President's federal budget proposal for Fiscal Year (FY) 2027. They highlighted that their plea represented the 1.2 million individuals in America who are affected by Parkinson's and related conditions.

data.title: Bitcoin's valuation likely reflects optimism regarding a swift end to Iran war. James Lavish, ex-hedge fund manager, sheds light.
data.description: During a recent interview featured on Cointelegraph, James Lavish, a macro investor, delves into the current market sentiment regarding the Iran war. Lavish elaborates on the reasoning behind the markets predicting a swift resolution to the conflict and the potential repercussions if this outlook proves to be incorrect.

data.title: Why Are Sigma Lithium Shares Trading Higher On Monday?
data.description: Shares of Sigma Lithium Corporation (NASDAQ: SGML) experienced an increase in value on Monday following the announcement of the restart of sales for its primary product after remobilizing mine operations. The company reported approximately $67 million in net sales for the fourth quarter FY25 and first quarter FY26, attributed to the sale of 650,000 tonnes of high-purity lithium fines and 5,000 tonnes of high-grade premium lithium oxide concentrate. Despite this positive news, the EPS loss was recorded at 22 cents, falling short of the consensus of five cents. Additionally, sales of $16.903 million were below the expected amount of $45.678 million. Sigma Lithium stock is soaring to record levels today, with investors showing enthusiasm for the company's latest developments.

data.title: The Inner Circle acknowledges John A. Wester as a Pinnacle Lifetime Achiever 2026
data.description: In Murray, Utah on April 3, 2026, John A. Wester has been recognized as a Pinnacle Lifetime Achiever 2026 for his notable work in Mental Health Advocacy and Veteran Support. Wester, a prominent figure in The Inner Circle, has dedicated his career to making a positive impact in these areas. This prestigious award highlights his significant contributions and efforts in supporting mental health and veterans.

data.title: The Inner Circle acknowledges Ilene Fennoy, MD, MPH acknowledged as a Pinnacle Professional Member Inner Circle of Excellence
data.description: In a recent feature in The Inner Circle, Dr. Ilene Fennoy, MD, MPH, has been recognized as a distinguished Pinnacle Professional Member of the Inner Circle of Excellence for her significant work in Pediatric Endocrinology and advocacy for child health. Dr. Fennoy's notable contributions have positioned her as a leading figure in her field, highlighting her expertise and dedication to improving pediatric health outcomes.
